name: Egg Foo Yung
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cooked or raw meat, sliced
2 cups Bean sprouts, cooked and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bean sprouts in water for about 7 minutes. Pour into a sieve and drain thoroughly. Canned bean sprouts may be used - merely drain them thoroughly.
2. Stir fry meat and onions in a wok until the meat is cooked. Put the mixture into a dish and allow it to cool.
3. When ingredients are cooled, mix them all together with 3 eggs, salt, and pepper in a small bowl. Do not overmix once the eggs have been added; a heavy hand will cause the eggs to become too liquid.
4. Add oil to a wok so that it reaches a depth of about 1 inch in the center. Turn the burner on medium setting and heat the oil for about 5 or 6 minutes. Ladle 1/3 of your omelette mixture into the wok. Cook for 5 minutes using a minute timer. Flip the omelette over and cook the reverse side for another 5 minutes. At the end of the cooking time, press down on the patties with a turner if raw egg oozes out; cooked patties will not have any egg leakage. Remove the cooked patties to a serving platter. Repeat the cooking procedure for the remaining two patties. Note: The oil for the second and third patties may become foamy, so lower the heat setting slightly. There should be enough oil initially in the wok so that additional oil is not needed for the last two patties; discard any remaining oil after cooking.
5. Arrange patties on a serving platter. (You may wish to serve gravy with patties. A recipe for gravy follows.)
NOTE: A minute timer is suggested for use in this recipe, as egg foo yung patties can easily break up in the wok if they do not receive sufficient cooking time.
